label: SES-025 v0.5-orientation closeout: 3 decisions, 1 session, 3 references (PI-001 PATCH handled separately by the apply prompt)

session.title: v0.5 orientation — engagement management workstream kickoff, PI-001 reopening, paper-test deferral
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai conversation on 05-16-26 that opened against the seed prompt 'Continued work on V2', surfaced the v1/v2 client-management overlap, committed v2 to building proper engagement management as v0.5, reopened PI-001 as a parallel workstream, and deferred the paper-test until v0.5 ships with a CBM engagement created. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: 'Continued work on V2' against the project default ARCHITECTURE operating mode.

Discovery walk through repository state. Read CLAUDE.md (684 lines, root), the v2 db-export JSON snapshots (status.json v13 'v0.4 complete', sessions.json with 24 entries, decisions.json with 74 entries, planning_items.json with 16 entries, plus references, charter, change_log), the four MVS methodology entity schema specs at methodology-schema-specs/, ui-PRD-v0.4.md, the workstream plans, the conduct framework at PRDs/process/conduct/, the evolved-methodology phase outline, the catalog ingestion artifacts, and the recent paper-test kickoff at methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md authored earlier same day.

State reconciliation. User memory was last updated around SES-011 (05-11-26) and was 5 days stale relative to actual state. Between SES-011 and conversation open: schema-design conversations SES-012–015 (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate), catalog ingestion SES-016 (42-entry base entity catalog into v2 database), v0.4-build-planning SES-017, v0.4 PRD reconciliation SES-018, v0.4 slices A–F as SES-019–024, conduct framework integration commits 2f47c20 + a5cb163, and the paper-test kickoff commit 75f2432. Status payload now reads 'phase: v0.4 complete'.

Surfacing of the actual confusion. Six candidate sources of confusion catalogued: memory drift, the unresolved coexistence of the current 13-phase Document Production Process and the evolved 5-phase methodology, three different meanings of 'entity' (methodology entity type / the methodology entity type literally named 'entity' / catalog entity), v1 and v2 not yet connected for the deployment path, PI-001 four-times-deferred styling, and the paper-test authored-but-unrun status. User pointed at the data-co-mingling problem: dogfooded v2.db is reserved for v2-build governance; testing CBM there would mix two unrelated bodies of data.

The DEC-039 multi-tenancy posture (one v2 instance per engagement; separate SQLite, separate API port; finding-not-decision) was rediscovered together with the operational mechanism gap (the code supports env-var-pointed alternate instances via CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH, CRMBUILDER_V2_API_PORT, CRMBUILDER_V2_API_BASE_URL, but no second instance has been created, no naming convention exists, and no runbook exists). The 'no second instance' state is exactly what would have been hit mid-Phase-1 if the paper-test had opened next.

Discovery of v1's existing data model. Read automation/db/master_schema.py (Client master DB with id/name/code/project_folder/crm_platform/deployment_model fields), automation/db/client_schema.py (31-table per-client SQLite schema at {project_folder}/.crmbuilder/{code}.db with Domain/Entity/Field/FieldOption/Relationship/Persona/BusinessObject/Process/ProcessStep/Requirement/Decision/AISession/ChangeLog/Layout*/Instance/DeploymentRun tables), automation/importer/ (25 files, Path B parsers and mappers for master_prd/business_object_discovery/domain_overview/domain_reconciliation/entity_prd/process_definition/crm_selection/crm_deployment/yaml_generation). Side-by-side comparison surfaced that v1 already has nearly every methodology entity type v2's deferred PIs (PI-003 persona, PI-004 field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec, PI-005 process_step) catalog as future work. v2's stated 'database as source of truth' rationale (DEC-001) is the same architectural goal v1 has been incrementally pursuing for two years.

Reframing from the user. The v2 plan was governance-first then incremental migration of v1 functionality onto v2's foundation. Engagement management is just the next migration item — not a deviation from the plan. PI-003/004/005 are similarly migration items, not new inventions.

The consequential decision settled. Path A (build proper Engagement management in v2 as v0.5) vs Path B (Frankenstein bridge reading v1's master.db) was weighed via the eight-element template. Costs are roughly equivalent (both require a registry mechanism, active-engagement context, switcher UI, launcher, tests). Path A produces no bridge code requiring eventual unwinding; Path B does. Path A wins on the consistent-with-plan and no-technical-debt dimensions. Path C (env-var override only, no UI) was named for completeness but not seriously considered.

v0.5 workstream shape proposed and approved. Three conversations: this v0.5-orientation conversation (produces the workstream plans and the paper-test deferral), Conversation 1 (combined multi-engagement architecture + engagement schema design, because architecture and schema are tightly coupled — engagement record field shape depends on routing decisions), Conversation 2 (v0.5 build planning produces PRD + implementation plan + Claude Code slice prompts). Then build slices A–D and closeout.

Ten architectural questions catalogued for Conversation 1 to settle: discovery model (bootstrap DB vs directory scan), per-engagement DB file location, active-engagement state persistence, API server model (multi-tenant vs one-process-per-engagement), identifier scope (global vs per-engagement), migrations across engagements, the dogfood-migration mechanism, per-engagement exports location, MCP server routing, engagement entity field set.

PI-001 disposition resolved. The four-deferral pattern with progressively-elaborate trigger conditions that never fire was named as an anti-pattern. CBM-redo-friction trigger from DEC-042 cannot fire in v0.5's timeframe either (CBM redo waits on v0.5 + paper-test + Phase 1). Two options weighed: defer a fifth time, or reopen as parallel work alongside v0.5. Reopening chosen because the work is independent of v0.5's data/routing layer, scheduling certainty exceeds clean release scoping, and Phase 1 (post-v0.5) lands on a coherent system rather than a Qt-default one. Boundary discipline named: styling owns visual layer (QSS, design tokens, palette, typography, spacing, panel chrome); v0.5 owns data/routing layer (config, ActiveEngagementContext, alembic, API routing, engagement entity). One coupling point: v0.5's engagement panel inherits whatever tokens are current at slice land time; either order ships fine.

Paper-test disposition resolved. The paper-test kickoff at methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md predates v0.5 and assumes one v2 instance. Running it now would land CBM domain content in the v2-build dogfood instance. Deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement is created. A 'Status: Deferred' blockquote header was added to the kickoff document; body content unchanged.

Close-out path chosen. Path 1 (Claude Code prompt at conversation close, mirroring v0.4's slice G precedent at commit 9cbf02d) selected over Path 2 (typing each record into the desktop New Session dialog). Path 1 produces a reviewable artifact; Path 2 was named for completeness.
session.artifacts_produced: Three documents committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ at commit ef365bc on origin/main: v0.5-engagement-management-workstream-plan.md (256 lines, new), styling-workstream-plan.md (227 lines, new), methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md (modified — Status: Deferred blockquote header added at top, body unchanged). After SES-025 was scheduled, three more artifacts were authored: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_025.json (this payload), PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-025.md (the apply prompt that delivers SES-025/DEC-075-077/PI-001 patch/references via Claude Code), PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-conversation-1-kickoff.md (seed prompt for the v0.5 architecture-plus-schema conversation), and PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-conversation-1-kickoff.md (seed prompt for the styling design pass conversation). Decisions DEC-075/076/077, PI-001 update, and three decided_in references land via this apply prompt; the apply commit captures the regenerated db-export snapshots.
session.in_flight_at_end: Two Conversation 1's queued in fresh Claude.ai chats: v0.5 Conversation 1 (against v0.5-conversation-1-kickoff.md, settles the ten architectural questions and produces multi-engagement-architecture.md + methodology-schema-specs/engagement.md) and styling Conversation 1 (against styling-conversation-1-kickoff.md, produces styling-design-pass.md). Either can open first; they run independently. After both Conversation 1's close, two Conversation 2's open: v0.5 build planning (produces ui-PRD-v0.5.md + implementation plan + slice prompts) and styling build planning (produces styling PRD + slice prompts plus settles the v0.5-bundle-or-v0.6-separate version question). Build slices follow per Claude Code. The paper-test stays deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record exists; the kickoff at methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md is annotated with the Status: Deferred header and requires light run-time editing when it eventually opens.

decisions.title: v0.5 release scope: add engagement management and multi-instance routing as v2-native capability, not a bridge to v1's Client master DB
decisions.context: v0.4 shipped four MVS methodology entity types (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate) into the v2 database, with the stated rationale of enabling CBM redo to use v2 as its system of record. v0.4 closeout exposed that those tables live in the same SQLite file (crmbuilder-v2/data/v2.db) that hosts the v2-build's own governance (24 sessions, 74 decisions, 16 PIs, etc.). Running CBM Phase 1 against the dogfood instance would co-mingle two unrelated bodies of data in one file with one identifier sequence. DEC-039's working answer to the multi-tenancy question — 'one v2 instance per engagement, separate SQLite, separate API port' — was framed as a finding rather than a designed feature; the operational mechanism (env-var-pointed alternate DB paths) existed in code but no second instance had been created, no naming convention existed, no UI for switching existed, no runbook existed. The v0.5-orientation conversation also surfaced that v1 already has multi-client management: automation/db/master_schema.py defines a Client master table; automation/ui/clients_tab.py ships a Clients tab; automation/db/client_schema.py defines a 31-table per-client methodology schema including Domain/Entity/Field/Persona/Process/ProcessStep/Requirement/Decision/AISession. The v2 charter (DEC-001 and surrounding) frames v2 as the next major iteration of CRMBuilder with v1 functionality migrating onto v2's foundation over time; engagement management is the natural next migration item.
decisions.decision: **v0.5 builds engagement management properly in v2 as the next migration item from v1.** v0.5 adds one new methodology entity type, `engagement` (or final name confirmed in Conversation 1), structured under MVS-thin scope per the v0.4 pattern: identifier + code (constrained to match v1's Client.code shape so the two systems can co-reference engagements by the same code) + name + purpose + status + audit columns. v0.5 also adds the supporting multi-engagement routing infrastructure: per-engagement SQLite databases, an active-engagement state mechanism, a bootstrap/discovery mechanism for enumerating engagements, an engagement-switcher mechanism in the desktop app, migration handling across engagements, and migration of the existing v2.db dogfood instance into the new engagement model. The workstream is structured as three conversations (this v0.5-orientation conversation; Conversation 1 combining multi-engagement architecture with engagement schema design; Conversation 2 producing v0.5 PRD and implementation plan); strawman four build slices follow. See PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-engagement-management-workstream-plan.md for the full plan.
decisions.rationale: Equal cost weighs the bridge-to-v1 option down. The Frankenstein path (Path B in the conversation) was named and weighed against building proper engagement management in v2 (Path A). Both paths require comparable work: a registry mechanism, an ActiveEngagementContext analog, a switcher UI or env-var workflow, a launcher, schema-compat handling, tests. The work cost is roughly equivalent. Path A produces no bridge code requiring eventual unwinding; Path B produces bridge code with a known sunset date (when v2 eventually builds its own engagement type). With cost equal, the path without technical debt wins. The migration framing also matters: every deferred v2 PI (PI-003 persona, PI-004 field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec, PI-005 process_step) is implicitly a migration item from v1's per-client schema. Engagement management is in the same category. Building it the v2 way once, at v0.5, is consistent with the broader plan; bridging now and reimplementing later is not.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Path B — Frankenstein bridge to v1's master.db. Rejected because the work cost is roughly equal to Path A and the path leaves bridge code that must be unwound when v2 eventually builds its own engagement type (which DEC-039's deferred PIs implicitly forecast). Path C — env-var override only, no UI, no v0.5 entity work. Rejected because it kicks the operational mechanism problem down the road without building the registry, the UI, or the migration; the env-var mechanism exists in code today and the gap is everything around it. Path A' — same as Path A but treated as a single-sprint focused build. Considered as a fallback if delay matters more than scope discipline; not the chosen posture because v0.5 is naturally bounded at one entity type plus routing infrastructure and a single-sprint frame doesn't materially compress that.
decisions.consequences: v0.5 inserts between v0.4 ship and any CBM activity. The paper-test conversation (DEC-077) and CBM redo Phase 1 both wait on v0.5 shipping plus a CBM engagement being created in v2. The v2.db dogfood instance migrates into the new engagement model — the 24 sessions, 74 decisions, 16 PIs, 66 references, 222 change_log entries, 13 status versions, 2 charter versions, and 42 catalog entities all survive the migration into a CRMBUILDER engagement record. Identifier sequences become per-engagement: CBM's first session is SES-001 within the CBM engagement, not SES-025+ in a global sequence. v0.5 does not pick up the deferred methodology PIs (PI-003 persona, PI-004 field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec, PI-005 process_step, PI-013 Cross-Domain Service, PI-014 catalog FK, PI-015 renderers, PI-016 router-vocab) — those remain v0.6+ candidates. The v2 charter's incremental-migration framing is reinforced rather than abandoned; engagement management is the next migration item from v1 in a planned sequence.

decisions.title: PI-001 (styling design pass) reopens as parallel workstream alongside v0.5, replacing fifth deferral
decisions.context: PI-001 was created in DEC-024 at v0.1 close on the principle that v2's early releases should ship structural capability first and visual polish later. Deferred three more times: DEC-026 at v0.2 close, DEC-037 at v0.3 close, DEC-042 at v0.4 close. DEC-042 added an explicit trigger mechanism: PI-001 would open ahead of any other v0.5 candidate if CBM-redo Phase 1 — running against the four methodology panels v0.4 delivered — surfaced visual friction on any of those panels. The v0.5-orientation conversation surfaced that this trigger cannot fire in v0.5's timeframe: CBM redo Phase 1 waits on v0.5 (DEC-075) plus the paper-test (DEC-077). The four-deferral pattern with progressively-elaborate trigger conditions that never fire is an anti-pattern; PI-001 has effectively become a placeholder rather than a real signal. Two paths: defer a fifth time with the same trigger that has never worked, or reopen as parallel work.
decisions.decision: **Reopen PI-001 as a parallel workstream alongside v0.5, executing independently of v0.5 build with its own kickoff, design pass, build planning, and slices.** Two-conversation structure mirroring v0.5's shape but smaller: Conversation 1 produces the design pass (palette, typography scale, spacing scale, radius/border/elevation tokens, density target, visual decisions for major component classes — output at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-design-pass.md); Conversation 2 produces the styling PRD and slice build prompts. Strawman five build slices follow: foundation (tokens module, base widget refactors, sidebar, About dialog), governance panel retrofit, methodology panel retrofit, dialog and form-control polish, closeout. PI-001's status changes from 'Open (deferred 4×)' to 'Open (active — parallel workstream)'. The version-bundling question (ship as v0.5 vs. ship as v0.6) is left for the styling workstream's Conversation 2 to settle. Boundary discipline between the two workstreams: styling owns the visual layer (QSS, design tokens, palette/typography/spacing, panel chrome, sidebar visuals, About dialog, hover/focus/disabled states); v0.5 owns the data/routing layer (config.py, ActiveEngagementContext, alembic-per-engagement, API server routing, engagement entity type, engagement-switcher mechanism). One real coupling point: v0.5's new engagement panel (slice B in the strawman) inherits whatever design tokens have shipped at the time the slice lands — either order ships fine. See PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-workstream-plan.md for the full plan.
decisions.rationale: Three things changed since the four-deferral pattern was established. First, the no-production-use condition is now structural rather than incidental — v2's only user is the v2 build itself, whose friction is largely with structural decisions (schema, routing, identifiers) rather than visual treatment. Even when CBM Phase 1 starts (post-v0.5), the data shape is thin enough that visual friction will be muted. Second, the system is no longer small — v0.4 ships sixteen panels across two sidebar groups, five dialogs, two scope-level concerns; designing a visual language now is comprehensive design, not a quick polish. Third, the deferral mechanism has become an anti-pattern — each deferral added another trigger condition; none have ever fired; the open PI has become a placeholder. Either the work is real and should be done, or PI-001 should be retired. The conversation concluded the former. Parallel execution rather than v0.5-then-styling-sequential is chosen because the work is independent of v0.5's data/routing layer (see boundary discipline), scheduling certainty exceeds clean release scoping at this point, and CBM Phase 1 (when it begins post-v0.5) benefits from landing on a coherent system rather than a Qt-default one.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Fifth deferral with the same CBM-redo-friction trigger. Rejected — the trigger has not fired in four cycles and cannot fire in v0.5's timeframe; the deferral is silent abandonment. Fifth deferral with a different trigger (e.g., post-Phase-1 review). Rejected for the same reason in different form — trigger conditions that cannot fire in the planning horizon don't constitute real deferral. v0.5-bundled — ship styling inside v0.5 rather than parallel. Rejected because engagement management and styling are independent capabilities that share no functional dependency; bundling mixes unrelated change in the README and complicates release notes. Sequential v0.5-then-styling — wait for v0.5 to ship before opening styling. Rejected because the work is independent enough that sequencing adds no clarity and parallel execution shortens the gap before CBM Phase 1 lands on a styled system. Retire PI-001 entirely — drop the styling work. Rejected — the work is real and the system needs visual coherence for Phase 1.
decisions.consequences: PI-001's open status persists but its meaning changes from 'deferred work waiting for a trigger' to 'active parallel workstream with its own plan.' Two Conversation 1's queue in fresh chats after this conversation closes — v0.5's and styling's — and can open in any order. The styling workstream has its own kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-conversation-1-kickoff.md and its own workstream plan at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-workstream-plan.md. Slice coordination at the engagement-panel coupling point is whichever-lands-first wins; the other rebases. Version-bundling decision deferred to the styling workstream's Conversation 2. PI-001's description is amended via PATCH to reflect reopening (see PI-001 in this apply).

decisions.title: Paper-test conversation deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record is created
decisions.context: The paper-test conversation kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md was authored 05-15-26 11:00 (commit 75f2432) and was framed as the next-up conversation after v0.4 closeout. The kickoff describes a structured validation conversation reading existing CBM domain content against the four v0.4 MVS schemas (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate) and producing two deliverables: a findings report and a single decision — 'ship CBM redo Phase 1 on v0.4 as-is' or 'amend N schemas first.' The kickoff predates v0.5 and assumes there is one v2 instance available to test against. DEC-075 inserts v0.5 between v0.4 ship and any CBM activity; running the paper-test now would land CBM domain content in the v2-build dogfood instance.
decisions.decision: **Defer the paper-test conversation until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record is created.** A 'Status: Deferred' blockquote header is added at the top of methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md naming the predecessor work (v0.5 ships; CBM engagement created in v2 with its own SQLite database; light run-time editing to reflect those changes). The kickoff body is unchanged because the paper-test's scope and method are unaffected — only the timing changes. The paper-test runs against the freshly-created CBM engagement, which is empty until CBM Phase 1 begins.
decisions.rationale: Running the paper-test against the dogfood instance would co-mingle CBM domain content with the v2-build's own governance content in one SQLite file with one identifier sequence — exactly the data-co-mingling problem v0.5 exists to solve (DEC-075). Deferring is the correct action; the alternative is doing the work twice (once now in the dogfood, again post-v0.5 in the proper CBM engagement) or accepting permanent co-mingling. The deferral is timing-only, not scope-or-method, so no kickoff body changes are required; a header note suffices.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Run the paper-test against the dogfood instance, accept co-mingling. Rejected — co-mingling is exactly what v0.5 prevents; defeating v0.5's purpose in advance defeats the rationale for the v0.5 detour. Run the paper-test against a manually-spun-up second v2 instance via env-var override before v0.5 ships. Rejected — the env-var mechanism exists but no second instance has been created, no naming convention exists, and the operational debt would still be incurred without v0.5's UI / registry / switching mechanism. Move the kickoff document to a 'deferred/' subfolder. Rejected as premature; a status header is sufficient and avoids breaking any pre-existing references to the path. Edit the kickoff body to anticipate v0.5. Rejected — anticipating v0.5 before v0.5 ships invents specifics the actual Conversation 1's might revise. Light run-time editing at the start of the paper-test conversation is the cleaner approach.
decisions.consequences: The paper-test conversation does not run next. It runs after v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record is created. The kickoff document is annotated with a Status: Deferred header that names the predecessor work; the document body remains identical. The light run-time editing required to reflect that v0.5 has shipped happens in the paper-test's actual opening conversation, not pre-emptively.
